# FocusMCP Benchmarks

Token-cost validation for the "200k → ~2k tokens" claim.
Auto-generated from `benchmarks/reports/summary.json`.

> Generated: 2026-04-23T20:47:36.791Z | Catalog: 2026-04-23T20:06:07.876Z | Bricks: 68 | Tokenizer: cl100k_base BPE (same as GPT-4/Claude)

## Headline

For typical agent tasks, FocusMCP reduces the tool-definition context by **~92.6% on average**
(best case: **93.4%** for "Find the entry point and explain the bootstrap flow").

| Task | Without FocusMCP | With FocusMCP | Savings |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Find the entry point and explain the bootstrap flow | 18,106 | 1,195 | **93.4%** |
| Find all usages of function X across the codebase | 18,106 | 1,593 | **91.2%** |
| Refactor module Y to use async/await | 18,106 | 1,257 | **93.1%** |

## Methodology

Each brick exposes its tools as a JSON array (name + description + inputSchema).
This is exactly what an MCP server sends in a `tools/list` response.
We serialize that array to JSON and count its BPE tokens using **cl100k_base BPE (same as GPT-4/Claude)**.

**Baseline** = sum of all 68 bricks' tool-definition tokens — the cost paid by an agent
that loads the full marketplace without FocusMCP.

**Focus** = sum of only the bricks required for a specific task — the cost paid when
FocusMCP selects the relevant subset at runtime.

**Savings** = (baseline − focus) / baseline × 100.

## Target repositories (Phase 2)

These 15 repositories will be cloned and benchmarked in Phase 2 (real execution).
No cloning is performed in Phase 1 — this is metadata only.

| Repo | Language | Profile | URL | Ref | LOC (est.) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---:|
| `next-js` | typescript | ssr-framework | [link](https://github.com/vercel/next.js) | `canary` | ~1,000,000 |
| `vscode` | typescript | electron-app | [link](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode) | `main` | ~1,500,000 |
| `nestjs` | typescript | backend-di | [link](https://github.com/nestjs/nest) | `master` | ~120,000 |
| `django` | python | web-framework | [link](https://github.com/django/django) | `main` | ~300,000 |
| `home-assistant` | python | large-app | [link](https://github.com/home-assistant/core) | `dev` | ~1,200,000 |
| `flask` | python | microframework | [link](https://github.com/pallets/flask) | `main` | ~15,000 |
| `kubernetes` | go | distributed | [link](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes) | `master` | ~2,500,000 |
| `grafana` | go | observability | [link](https://github.com/grafana/grafana) | `main` | ~800,000 |
| `tokio` | rust | async-runtime | [link](https://github.com/tokio-rs/tokio) | `master` | ~80,000 |
| `deno` | rust | runtime | [link](https://github.com/denoland/deno) | `main` | ~500,000 |
| `spring-boot` | java | enterprise | [link](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot) | `main` | ~500,000 |
| `elasticsearch` | java | search-engine | [link](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch) | `main` | ~1,800,000 |
| `laravel` | php | modern-mvc | [link](https://github.com/laravel/framework) | `master` | ~200,000 |
| `symfony` | php | enterprise-di | [link](https://github.com/symfony/symfony) | `7.x` | ~600,000 |
| `wordpress` | php | legacy-hooks | [link](https://github.com/WordPress/WordPress) | `master` | ~250,000 |

## Reproducing

```bash
pnpm benchmark
```

This runs:
1. `tsx benchmarks/src/measure.ts` — reads every `bricks/*/mcp-brick.json`, counts tokens, writes `benchmarks/reports/summary.json` and per-task reports.
2. `tsx benchmarks/src/report.ts` — reads `summary.json`, writes this file (`BENCHMARKS.md`).

## Limitations

- **Phase 1 only**: measures static token cost of tool definitions, not runtime context windows.
- **No input data**: does not count tokens from actual file contents fed to the agent during a task.
- **Tokenizer**: cl100k_base BPE (same as GPT-4/Claude). Counts may differ slightly from actual model billing.
- **Brick selection**: the `required_bricks` sets in `benchmarks/tasks.json` are manually curated expert estimates, not measured from real agent sessions.
- **Phase 2** will add real repo execution: clone target repos, run each task scenario with and without FocusMCP, and measure actual context window usage.
